mechanism: Fiscal deficits are the single cause producing both effects: earnings support (via Kalecki-Levy identity flowing ~$400-600B to corporate profits) and term premium elevation (via $2T+ Treasury issuance). The two effects operate on different timescales — earnings support is immediate while term premium rebuilds over quarters.
regime_dependence: Holds as long as deficits remain elevated (>4.5% GDP) and the central bank is not absorbing net issuance. Neutralized under large-scale QE as demonstrated by Japan.

mechanism: IG credit spread widening >75bp from trough has preceded 6/7 post-1960 correlation regime transitions with an 18-month lead. The credit market reprices first because it sits at the intersection of equity risk (default probability) and rates risk (duration and financing).
regime_dependence: Most reliable when not masked by covenant-lite structures and private credit opacity, which may extend lead time to 24-36 months in current environment.

mechanism: Covenant-lite structures, EBITDA addbacks, and private credit opacity delay the onset of credit spread widening by suppressing default recognition and enabling extend-and-pretend. This extends the credit leading indicator's lead time from 18 to potentially 24-36 months.
regime_dependence: The delay is structural and persistent in the current credit market structure (>90% covenant-lite). Would be reduced if covenant structures tightened in a new issuance environment.

mechanism: The basis trade functions as synthetic QE, removing duration from private markets and compressing measured term premium by 15-25bp below fair value. Its unwind would release suppressed term premium, with partial unwind (20-40%) adding ~5-10bp and full unwind potentially adding 15-25bp.
regime_dependence: Active as long as the trade is profitable (positive cash-futures basis) and repo financing is available. Vulnerable to margin calls, regulatory action, or funding market stress.

mechanism: While Kalecki-Levy aggregate profit support may sustain overall corporate health, the quality differentiation means leveraged credits facing the maturity wall cannot access this aggregate floor. Refinancing at 200-400bp higher costs is lethal at the firm level regardless of aggregate profits.
regime_dependence: Most acute during coincident recession when both aggregate profits are at risk (reduced fiscal transfer) and individual firm cash flows are stressed.

mechanism: As CLO equity IRRs compress toward the 11-12% threshold, new CLO formation slows, removing ~65-70% of institutional leveraged loan demand. This constrains refinancing capacity exactly when $900B-$1.5T of sub-IG debt needs to roll, creating a credit supply squeeze.
regime_dependence: Binding when CLO equity IRRs are near or below threshold AND maturity wall refinancing needs are concentrated. Currently in early stages of potential convergence.

mechanism: The 4:1 timing asymmetry (fast negative transitions, slow positive transitions) means hedges against negative-correlation snaps must be pre-positioned (no time to react) while positive-correlation exposure can be built gradually as signals develop. This directly supports barbell construction with convex tail hedges.
regime_dependence: Universal across all 13 post-1960 transitions. The asymmetry implies a structural portfolio construction principle regardless of which transition is expected.

mechanism: The Merton-model credit-equity basis (implied IG ~140-180bp vs. actual ~90-110bp) is the quantitative expression of the broader cross-asset pricing disagreement as seen through the equity-credit lens. The widest gap since 2007 implies credit is pricing a fundamentally different world than equity volatility suggests.
regime_dependence: The basis has historically resolved via credit widening (5/7 episodes). Resolution requires a catalyst — typically recession, financial stress, or forced recognition event.

mechanism: The shift from ~+$300-500B/year official-sector Treasury purchases to approximately flat removes a structural buyer, adding an estimated 20-40bp to term premium via Krishnamurthy-Vissing-Jorgensen supply elasticity. This is additive to fiscal issuance pressure.
regime_dependence: Structural shift unlikely to reverse given geopolitical bifurcation and declining petrodollar recycling. Would reverse only under major geopolitical realignment or new reserve accumulation cycle.

mechanism: Fallen angel events ($200-400B BBB- at risk) convert gradual credit deterioration into discrete correlation-flipping events through mechanical IG-mandated forced selling that overwhelms HY absorption capacity ($50-100B). Each historical instance (2002, 2005, 2020) coincided with negative correlation shifts.
regime_dependence: Requires sufficient concentration of BBB- debt near downgrade triggers AND a macro catalyst. Currently building vulnerability but no imminent trigger.

mechanism: Fiscal dominance captures the long end of the yield curve, making 30Y correlation with equities structurally positive (both driven by fiscal dynamics) while the front end remains monetary-policy-driven and negatively correlated. This produces the observed maturity-dependent bifurcation.
regime_dependence: Requires persistent deficits above ~4.5% GDP AND the central bank not absorbing net issuance. Currently both conditions are met. Would be disrupted by fiscal consolidation or renewed large-scale QE.

mechanism: The current fiscal deficit (~6.5% GDP) exceeds all six historical precedents of fiscal conditioning, which showed median ~3-year extensions. Combined with the Conundrum analogue's sequencing template (30 months accumulation → 12 months contained → 6 months discontinuous), this suggests the current regime may have an extended Phase 3 before resolution.
regime_dependence: Holds as long as fiscal deficits remain elevated and no exogenous forcing event occurs. Historical pattern required either recession or overwhelming central bank intervention for resolution.

mechanism: If the S&P 493 is already in positive correlation territory while the cap-weighted index appears to oscillate near zero, the true required ERP for the broader market is higher than headline measures suggest. The mega-cap concentration masks the regime-conditional overvaluation of the equal-weight market.
regime_dependence: Depends on the unverified hypothesis that S&P 493 correlation is materially more positive than cap-weighted. Becomes more relevant as concentration increases.

mechanism: Chinese geopolitically motivated selling (from ~$1.08T to ~$770B, accelerating under stress) is the single largest component of the reserve manager de-accumulation trend. Unlike Japanese economically motivated selling, it is irreversible under current geopolitical conditions.
regime_dependence: Accelerates under US-China tensions. Would slow or reverse only under major geopolitical de-escalation, which appears unlikely through the medium term.

mechanism: The dollar's position on the smile provides a real-time discriminator for the correlation regime: risk-off dollar strength corresponds to negative correlation (demand shocks dominant), while US exceptionalism dollar strength corresponds to positive correlation (supply shocks/fiscal dominance). DXY at 100-107 maps to the transitional middle.
regime_dependence: The mechanism behind any dollar break matters more than the level. Recession-driven weakness (EM outflows) vs. twin-deficit-driven weakness (EM inflows) discriminates between correlation regime states.

mechanism: The 3m10y un-inversion occurred primarily (~75%) from term premium expansion rather than changed growth expectations, degrading the spread's recession-prediction reliability. Both effects stem from the same root cause: fiscal supply pressure elevating term premium at the long end while the front end remains policy-anchored.
regime_dependence: Active whenever term premium is rising due to fiscal supply. Was not operative in the low/stable term premium era when the 3m10y model was calibrated.

mechanism: The Goldilocks conjunction (multiple conditions simultaneously satisfied) is what allows cross-asset pricing disagreement to persist — each market can find its own reason for optimism within the conjunction. When any condition fails, the disagreement resolves, likely through credit first.
regime_dependence: Fragility increases as the conjunction duration extends, since each condition becomes statistically less likely to persist. Currently 3+ years into the conjunction.

mechanism: High vol-of-correlation (oscillation between -0.30 and +0.30 on 3-6 month frequency) whipsaws mean-optimized portfolios while rewarding barbell construction that is convex in both tails. The reflexive amplification through vol-targeting strategies ($500B-$1T AUM) makes the oscillation self-reinforcing.
regime_dependence: Active during the current oscillating/transitional regime. Would diminish if correlation stabilized in either direction for an extended period.
